archetype: ARCHETYPE-4 (Public DSL / Builder) — the primary product of `@netscript/service` is the fluent `createService(...).with*().serve()` builder; this slice adds builder methods (`onShutdown`), a new `ServeOptions` field, and listener-lifecycle behavior, which is squarely DSL/builder surface work. Runtime gates apply because the builder materializes a long-running listener (per ARCHETYPE-4 "if the builder materializes a long-running runtime, add the relevant runtime gates").
lockedDecisions: Add graceful drain to RunningService.stop(): abort internal controller, call Deno.serve server.shutdown(), run LIFO shutdown hooks under drainTimeoutMs, await server.finished. stop() KEEPS its Promise<void> return type (no breaking change); ShutdownReport is internal + logged, not returned.

Add ServiceBuilder.onShutdown(hook: ShutdownHook) mirroring onStartup; hooks run LIFO during stop() and on signal-triggered shutdown; hook failures are collected (not thrown) so one bad hook cannot strand the rest.

Signal handling lives in service-listener.ts via Deno.addSignalListener for SIGINT/SIGTERM (POSIX) and SIGBREAK (Windows only), platform-guarded by Deno.build.os; listeners removed in teardown finally to avoid leaks across serve/stop cycles.
Signal handling is ON by default, suppressible via serve({ handleSignals: false }) for embedding/test hosts.
ServeOptions gains drainTimeoutMs (default 30_000, matching ShutdownManager) and handleSignals (default true).

New ServiceShutdownCoordinator lives in packages/service/src/builder/service-shutdown.ts as a package-PRIVATE helper (NOT exported from mod.ts); it mirrors plugin-workers-core ShutdownManager's pattern but takes NO dependency on the plugin (layering law).
defineService() registers a real DB $disconnect shutdown hook when the resolved health-check client exposes $disconnect; capability-guarded, no no-op.

All new types (ShutdownReason, ShutdownContext, ShutdownHook, ShutdownHookOutcome, ShutdownReport) are package-owned structural types in types.ts, re-exported from existing mod.ts type block — no new subpath, no upstream/Zod re-export.
plugins/streams/services/src/main.ts hand-rolled signal handling is NOT refactored in this slice (recorded as follow-up drift); no e2e:cli run since no scaffold output changes.
contracts: ServeOptions extended with drainTimeoutMs?: number (default 30_000) and handleSignals?: boolean (default true) in packages/service/src/types.ts
New interface ShutdownContext { readonly reason: ShutdownReason; readonly signal?: Deno.Signal }
New type ShutdownHook = (context: ShutdownContext) => Promise<void> | void
New interface ShutdownHookOutcome { readonly ok: boolean; readonly error?: string }
New interface ShutdownReport { readonly reason: ShutdownReason; readonly timedOut: boolean; readonly hooks: readonly ShutdownHookOutcome[] } (internal/logged, not returned by stop())
ServiceBuilder<TRouter> interface gains onShutdown(hook: ShutdownHook): ServiceBuilder<TRouter> in packages/service/src/builder/service-builder.ts
Package-private ServiceShutdownCoordinator with runShutdown(reason, signal?): Promise<ShutdownReport> in packages/service/src/builder/service-shutdown.ts (not in mod.ts public surface)
RunningService.stop() contract strengthened: graceful drain (abort controller + server.shutdown() + LIFO hooks + server.finished), idempotent; signature stays Promise<void>
testPlan: Unit (shutdown-coordinator_test.ts): hooks run in LIFO registration order; a throwing hook is captured as ShutdownHookOutcome{ok:false,error} and does NOT prevent later hooks; runShutdown() is idempotent (second call returns the same report, hooks run once); drainTimeoutMs elapsed sets report.timedOut=true while still resolving.

Integration (runtime_test.ts): serve({ port:0 }) then stop() drains an in-flight slow request to completion (request started before stop resolves with 200, listener closed after) — proves graceful server.shutdown() vs abrupt abort.
Integration (runtime_test.ts): registered onShutdown hooks all execute exactly once on stop(); fetch after stop() rejects with TypeError (listener closed).

Integration (runtime_test.ts): serve({ handleSignals:false }) installs no signal listeners (no leak); default serve installs and then removes signal listeners across a serve/stop cycle (no duplicate-handler error on a second serve in the same process).
Failure-path: a shutdown hook that rejects is collected and logged; stop() still resolves and the server still closes (no unhandled rejection, no hang).
Failure-path: drainTimeoutMs honored — a hook that never resolves causes stop() to resolve at the timeout with report.timedOut=true rather than hanging the process.
Preset (define-service): when a $disconnect-capable client is supplied, stop() invokes client.$disconnect() exactly once; when not capable, no disconnect hook is registered (no throw).
Back-compat: existing runtime_test.ts cases (ephemeral port, external-signal abort, invalid port, startup-hook failure, error response, address) still pass unchanged with stop() as Promise<void>.
Consumer: deno check of plugins/workers/services and plugins/sagas/services entrypoints still compiles against the additive builder surface.
planGateSelfCheck: PASS expected on all 8 boxes. (1) Research present & current — every cited file:line re-verified on main@cc3b8731 with current line numbers; no gap already resolved (both blockers confirmed unresolved). (2) Decisions locked — 9 locked decisions with rationale (graceful drain mechanism, onShutdown LIFO, platform-guarded signals, default-on suppressible, coordinator as private helper not plugin dep, stop() signature preserved). (3) Open-decision sweep — only open items are safe-to-defer (streams refactor, plugin composition-root wiring) with no rework risk to this slice's surface; no deferred decision forces later rework. (4) Commit slices — 6 ordered, <30, each names what it proves + gate + files. (5) Risk register — provided with mitigations. (6) Gate set — selected from archetype-gate-matrix for ARCHETYPE-4 + SCOPE-service overlay, runtime gates scoped out with justification (no scaffold change). (7) Deferred scope explicit — streams hand-roll + plugin wiring + auth. (8) jsr-audit — applied to planned surface: additive package-owned structural types, no slow types, no upstream/Zod re-export, @example on new methods → publishable; named explicitly. Residual FAIL_PLAN risk: low — main risk is a reviewer wanting stop() to RETURN ShutdownReport; mitigated by locking stop():Promise<void> for back-compat and surfacing the report via hooks+logs.
openQuestions: Should stop() expose the ShutdownReport (e.g. via a new stopWithReport() or RunningService.lastShutdown) for programmatic inspection? LOCKED as deferred: report is logged + passed to hooks; adding a return value can be a later additive change with zero rework to this slice. Safe to defer.

Should signal-triggered shutdown also call Deno.exit() after drain? LOCKED: NO — the listener stops and frees the process naturally; calling Deno.exit() would prevent clean test teardown and other top-level shutdown hooks. Safe to defer / out of scope.
risks: Windows signal landmine: Deno.addSignalListener('SIGTERM'|'SIGBREAK') throws on the wrong OS. Mitigation: platform-guard via Deno.build.os (POSIX → SIGINT+SIGTERM; Windows → SIGINT+SIGBREAK); unit/integration cover handleSignals:false to avoid registering signals in CI where SIGTERM is delivered by the test runner.

Signal-listener leak across serve/stop cycles (tests serve repeatedly in one process). Mitigation: removeSignalListener in stop()'s finally; test asserts a second serve in the same process does not throw a duplicate-handler error.

Graceful drain could hang if a user hook never resolves. Mitigation: drainTimeoutMs (default 30_000) with Promise.race timeout accounting; report.timedOut surfaced + logged; failure-path test proves stop() resolves at timeout.

Changing stop() from abrupt abort to graceful server.shutdown() could alter timing assumptions in existing tests/consumers. Mitigation: keep abort of the internal controller too (belt-and-suspenders), preserve Promise<void> signature, and re-run full packages/service/tests plus consumer deno check.
Layering temptation to import plugin-workers-core ShutdownManager. Mitigation: locked decision to reimplement the pattern as a package-private coordinator; F-3 layering check stays green.
Scope creep into streams/plugin composition roots. Mitigation: explicitly deferred + recorded as drift; this slice ships only the @netscript/service seam.
